blockingIssues.description: useOrchestratorStatus uses raw fetch instead of OrchestratorClient, diverging from shared-client requirement and bypassing common auth/error behavior.
Tab focus cycling between input bar and sidebar is not implemented; only sidebar is focus-enabled so required panel focus switching is missing.
AgentSidebar lacks scrolling/windowing behavior for long lists; arrow-key selection exists but no visible-window offset logic is implemented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen the panel milestone procedure to explicitly require OrchestratorClient usage for orchestrator status access (not raw fetch) and to require a red-green test sequence callout for keyboard interaction features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature tui-agent-sidebar-header-status implemented panel polling via raw fetch despite AGENTS guidance and delivered weak keyboard behavior tests that did not enforce Tab focus-cycle and list-scroll requirements.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a reviewer checklist item to verify handoff `followedProcedure` claims against transcript ordering when tests are expected to be written first.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In tui-agent-sidebar-header-status, transcript skeleton indicates implementation started before key tests were added, while handoff still reported followedProcedure=true.
